members.name: GetCameraFeedId
members.summary: If this is an AR interface that requires displaying a camera feed as the background, this method returns the feed ID in the [CameraServer] for this interface.

members.name: GetCapabilities
members.summary: Returns a combination of [enum Capabilities] flags providing information about the capabilities of this interface.

members.name: GetName
members.summary: Returns the name of this interface ([code]"OpenXR"[/code], [code]"OpenVR"[/code], [code]"OpenHMD"[/code], [code]"ARKit"[/code], etc.).

members.name: GetPlayArea
members.summary: Returns an array of vectors that represent the physical play area mapped to the virtual space around the [XROrigin3D] point. The points form a convex polygon that can be used to react to or visualize the play area. This returns an empty array if this feature is not supported or if the information is not yet available.

members.name: GetProjectionForView
members.signature: public Projection GetProjectionForView(int view, float aspect, float near, float far)
members.summary: Returns the projection matrix for a view/eye.

members.name: GetRenderTargetSize
members.summary: Returns the resolution at which we should render our intermediate results before things like lens distortion are applied by the VR platform.

members.name: GetSupportedEnvironmentBlendModes
members.summary: Returns the an array of supported environment blend modes, see [enum XRInterface.EnvironmentBlendMode].

members.name: GetSystemInfo
members.summary: Returns a [Dictionary] with extra system info. Interfaces are expected to return [code]XRRuntimeName[/code] and [code]XRRuntimeVersion[/code] providing info about the used XR runtime. Additional entries may be provided specific to an interface. [b]Note:[/b]This information may only be available after [method initialize] was successfully called.

members.name: GetTrackingStatus
members.summary: If supported, returns the status of our tracking. This will allow you to provide feedback to the user whether there are issues with positional tracking.

members.name: GetTransformForView
members.signature: public Transform3D GetTransformForView(int view, Transform3D camTransform)
members.summary: Returns the transform for a view/eye. [param view] is the view/eye index. [param cam_transform] is the transform that maps device coordinates to scene coordinates, typically the [member Node3D.global_transform] of the current XROrigin3D.

members.name: GetViewCount
members.summary: Returns the number of views that need to be rendered for this device. 1 for Monoscopic, 2 for Stereoscopic.

members.name: Initialize
members.summary: Call this to initialize this interface. The first interface that is initialized is identified as the primary interface and it will be used for rendering output. After initializing the interface you want to use you then need to enable the AR/VR mode of a viewport and rendering should commence. [b]Note:[/b] You must enable the XR mode on the main viewport for any device that uses the main output of Godot, such as for mobile VR. If you do this for a platform that handles its own output (such as OpenVR) Godot will show just one eye without distortion on screen. Alternatively, you can add a separate viewport node to your scene and enable AR/VR on that viewport. It will be used to output to the HMD, leaving you free to do anything you like in the main window, such as using a separate camera as a spectator camera or rendering something completely different. While currently not used, you can activate additional interfaces. You may wish to do this if you want to track controllers from other platforms. However, at this point in time only one interface can render to an HMD.

members.name: IsInitialized
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this interface has been initialized.

members.name: IsPassthroughEnabled
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if passthrough is enabled.

members.name: IsPassthroughSupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this interface supports passthrough.

members.name: SetEnvironmentBlendMode
members.summary: Sets the active environment blend mode. [param mode] is the environment blend mode starting with the next frame. [b]Note:[/b] Not all runtimes support all environment blend modes, so it is important to check this at startup. For example: [codeblock] func _ready(): var xr_interface = XRServer.find_interface("OpenXR") if xr_interface and xr_interface.is_initialized(): var vp = get_viewport() vp.use_xr = true var acceptable_modes = [XRInterface.XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_OPAQUE, XRInterface.XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_ADDITIVE] var modes = xr_interface.get_supported_environment_blend_modes() for mode in acceptable_modes: if mode in modes: xr_interface.set_environment_blend_mode(mode) break [/codeblock]

members.name: SetPlayAreaMode
members.summary: Sets the active play area mode, will return [code]false[/code] if the mode can't be used with this interface. [b]Note:[/b] Changing this after the interface has already been initialized can be jarring for the player, so it's recommended to recenter on the HMD with [method XRServer.center_on_hmd] (if switching to [constant XRInterface.XR_PLAY_AREA_STAGE]) or make the switch during a scene change.

members.name: StartPassthrough
members.summary: Starts passthrough, will return [code]false[/code] if passthrough couldn't be started. [b]Note:[/b] The viewport used for XR must have a transparent background, otherwise passthrough may not properly render.

members.name: StopPassthrough
members.summary: Stops passthrough.

members.name: SupportsPlayAreaMode
members.summary: Call this to find out if a given play area mode is supported by this interface.

members.name: TriggerHapticPulse
members.signature: public void TriggerHapticPulse(String actionName, StringName trackerName, float frequency, float amplitude, float durationSec, float delaySec)
members.summary: Triggers a haptic pulse on a device associated with this interface. [param action_name] is the name of the action for this pulse. [param tracker_name] is optional and can be used to direct the pulse to a specific device provided that device is bound to this haptic. [param frequency] is the frequency of the pulse, set to [code]0.0[/code] to have the system use a default frequency. [param amplitude] is the amplitude of the pulse between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [param duration_sec] is the duration of the pulse in seconds. [param delay_sec] is a delay in seconds before the pulse is given.

members.name: Uninitialize
members.summary: Turns the interface off.

members.name: ArIsAnchorDetectionEnabled
members.summary: On an AR interface, [code]true[/code] if anchor detection is enabled.

members.name: EnvironmentBlendMode
members.summary: Specify how XR should blend in the environment. This is specific to certain AR and passthrough devices where camera images are blended in by the XR compositor.

members.name: InterfaceIsPrimary
members.summary: [code]true[/code] if this is the primary interface.

members.name: XrPlayAreaMode
members.summary: The play area mode for this interface.

signals.name: PlayAreaChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the play area is changed. This can be a result of the player resetting the boundary or entering a new play area, the player changing the play area mode, the world scale changing or the player resetting their headset orientation.

values.name: XR_AR
values.summary: This interface supports AR (video background and real world tracking).

values.name: XR_EXTERNAL
values.summary: This interface outputs to an external device. If the main viewport is used, the on screen output is an unmodified buffer of either the left or right eye (stretched if the viewport size is not changed to the same aspect ratio of [method get_render_target_size]). Using a separate viewport node frees up the main viewport for other purposes.

values.name: XR_MONO
values.summary: This interface can work with normal rendering output (non-HMD based AR).

values.name: XR_NONE
values.summary: No XR capabilities.

values.name: XR_QUAD
values.summary: This interface supports quad rendering (not yet supported by Godot).

values.name: XR_STEREO
values.summary: This interface supports stereoscopic rendering.

values.name: XR_VR
values.summary: This interface supports VR.

values.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_ADDITIVE
values.summary: Additive blend mode. This is typically used for AR devices or VR devices with passthrough.

values.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_ALPHA_BLEND
values.summary: Alpha blend mode. This is typically used for AR or VR devices with passthrough capabilities. The alpha channel controls how much of the passthrough is visible. Alpha of 0.0 means the passthrough is visible and this pixel works in ADDITIVE mode. Alpha of 1.0 means that the passthrough is not visible and this pixel works in OPAQUE mode.

values.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_OPAQUE
values.summary: Opaque blend mode. This is typically used for VR devices.

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_3DOF
values.summary: Play area only supports orientation tracking, no positional tracking, area will center around player.

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_CUSTOM
values.summary: Custom play area set by a GDExtension.

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_ROOMSCALE
values.summary: Player is free to move around, full positional tracking.

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_SITTING
values.summary: Player is in seated position, limited positional tracking, fixed guardian around player.

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_STAGE
values.summary: Same as [constant XR_PLAY_AREA_ROOMSCALE] but origin point is fixed to the center of the physical space. In this mode, system-level recentering may be disabled, requiring the use of [method XRServer.center_on_hmd].

values.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_UNKNOWN
values.summary: Play area mode not set or not available.

values.name: XR_EXCESSIVE_MOTION
values.summary: Tracking is hindered by excessive motion (the player is moving faster than tracking can keep up).

values.name: XR_INSUFFICIENT_FEATURES
values.summary: Tracking is hindered by insufficient features, it's too dark (for camera-based tracking), player is blocked, etc.

values.name: XR_NORMAL_TRACKING
values.summary: Tracking is behaving as expected.

values.name: XR_NOT_TRACKING
values.summary: Tracking is not functional (camera not plugged in or obscured, lighthouses turned off, etc.).

values.name: XR_UNKNOWN_TRACKING
values.summary: We don't know the status of the tracking or this interface does not provide feedback.

values.name: XR_VRS_TEXTURE_FORMAT_FRAGMENT_DENSITY_MAP
values.summary: The texture format is the same as expected by the Vulkan [code]VK_EXT_fragment_density_map[/code] extension.

values.name: XR_VRS_TEXTURE_FORMAT_FRAGMENT_SHADING_RATE
values.summary: The texture format is the same as expected by the Vulkan [code]VK_KHR_fragment_shading_rate[/code] extension.

values.name: XR_VRS_TEXTURE_FORMAT_UNIFIED
values.summary: The texture format is the same as returned by [method XRVRS.make_vrs_texture].

constants.name: XR_NONE
constants.summary: No XR capabilities.

constants.name: XR_STEREO

If the main viewport is used, the on screen output is an unmodified buffer of either the left or right eye (stretched if the viewport size is not changed to the same aspect ratio of [method get_render_target_size]). Using a separate viewport node frees up the main viewport for other purposes.

constants.summary: Tracking is hindered by excessive motion (the player is moving faster than tracking can keep up).

constants.summary: Play area only supports orientation tracking, no positional tracking, area will center around player.

constants.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_SITTING
constants.summary: Player is in seated position, limited positional tracking, fixed guardian around player.

constants.summary: Player is free to move around, full positional tracking.

constants.name: XR_PLAY_AREA_STAGE
constants.summary: Same as [constant XR_PLAY_AREA_ROOMSCALE] but origin point is fixed to the center of the physical space. In this mode, system-level recentering may be disabled, requiring the use of [method XRServer.center_on_hmd].

constants.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_OPAQUE
constants.summary: Opaque blend mode. This is typically used for VR devices.

constants.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_ADDITIVE
constants.summary: Additive blend mode. This is typically used for AR devices or VR devices with passthrough.

constants.name: XR_ENV_BLEND_MODE_ALPHA_BLEND
constants.summary: Alpha blend mode. This is typically used for AR or VR devices with passthrough capabilities. The alpha channel controls how much of the passthrough is visible. Alpha of 0.0 means the passthrough is visible and this pixel works in ADDITIVE mode. Alpha of 1.0 means that the passthrough is not visible and this pixel works in OPAQUE mode.

constants.name: XR_VRS_TEXTURE_FORMAT_UNIFIED
constants.summary: The texture format is the same as returned by [method XRVRS.make_vrs_texture].

constants.name: XR_VRS_TEXTURE_FORMAT_FRAGMENT_SHADING_RATE
constants.summary: The texture format is the same as expected by the Vulkan [code]VK_KHR_fragment_shading_rate[/code] extension.

constants.summary: The texture format is the same as expected by the Vulkan [code]VK_EXT_fragment_density_map[/code] extension.
